req.title: Manager updates department
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to update a department through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that an authenticated manager can update a department via REST API, mandatory fields are validated, and an audit record is written on success. Tests derived: (1) positive EP test confirming a valid update returns 200/201, entity id is present, and audit record exists; (2) negative NEG test confirming that a missing mandatory field returns 400; (3) boundary BVA test exercising the minimum-length department name (1 character) to probe the lower bound; (4) negative DT test covering the unauthenticated path (401) — DT is appropriate because the decision node is 'token present vs absent'; (5) negative EP test verifying that a non-manager role receives 403 — changed from STC to EP because the forbidden-role scenario is a distinct equivalence partition of the role input domain (role != manager), not a state-transition event. This fixes the violation: test[4] was kind=negative with heuristic=STC, which is illogical because STC applies to state-machine coverage, not role-partition checks. All tests remain idempotent through fixture-managed setup/teardown.

tests.name: test_manager_updates_department_valid_payload
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A department with known id exists
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token>
Create or retrieve a target department via <ensure_department_exists>
PATCH /api/departments/{id} with all mandatory fields populated and valid values
Query the audit log via <get_latest_audit_record> for the department id
expected: Response body contains the updated entity id
Department name in response matches submitted value
Audit record exists for this department update
trace: R-WEB-006: authenticated manager can update a department
R-WEB-006: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-006: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_updates_department_valid_payload(
    manager_token: str,
    api_client: "<fixture>",
    ensure_department_exists: "<fixture>",
    get_latest_audit_record: "<helper_fn>",
):
    dept = ensure_department_exists(name="Original Dept EP")
    dept_id = dept["id"]
    payload = {"name": "Updated Dept EP", "cost_center": "CC-001"}

    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/api/departments/{dept_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_token}"},
    )

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(dept_id)
    assert body.get("name") == "Updated Dept EP"

    audit = get_latest_audit_record(entity="department", entity_id=dept_id)
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record not found"
    assert audit.get("action") == "update"

tests.name: test_manager_update_department_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A department with known id exists
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token>
Create or retrieve a target department via <ensure_department_exists>
PATCH /api/departments/{id} with the mandatory 'name' field omitted from the payload
expected: Response body contains a validation error message referencing the missing field
trace: R-WEB-006: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_update_department_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(
    manager_token: str,
    api_client: "<fixture>",
    ensure_department_exists: "<fixture>",
):
    dept = ensure_department_exists(name="Dept NEG Missing Field")
    dept_id = dept["id"]
    payload = {"cost_center": "CC-002"}  # 'name' intentionally omitted

    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/api/departments/{dept_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_token}"},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    errors = str(body).lower()
    assert "name" in errors, "Validation error should reference missing 'name' field"

tests.name: test_manager_update_department_name_minimum_length_boundary
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A department with known id exists
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token>
Create or retrieve a target department via <ensure_department_exists>
PATCH /api/departments/{id} with 'name' set to exactly 1 character (lower bound)
If 400, verify validation error; if 200/201, verify entity id returned
expected: Response status is either 200/201 (if 1-char name is accepted) or 400 (if minimum length > 1)
Response body is consistent with the status (entity id on success, validation error on failure)
trace: R-WEB-006: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-006: boundary of minimum department name length
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_update_department_name_minimum_length_boundary(
    manager_token: str,
    api_client: "<fixture>",
    ensure_department_exists: "<fixture>",
):
    dept = ensure_department_exists(name="Dept BVA Min Length")
    dept_id = dept["id"]
    payload = {"name": "X"}  # 1-character: lower boundary

    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/api/departments/{dept_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_token}"},
    )

    assert resp.status_code < 500, f"Server error on boundary input: {resp.status_code}"
    if resp.status_code in (200, 201):
        body = resp.json()
        assert str(body.get("id")) == str(dept_id)
    else:
        assert resp.status_code == 400, (
            f"Expected 400 for invalid boundary, got {resp.status_code}"
        )

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_update_department_returns_401
preconditions: A department with known id exists
actions: Create or retrieve a target department id via <ensure_department_exists> using an admin token
expected: Response body contains an authentication error indication
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_update_department_returns_401(
    api_client: "<fixture>",
    ensure_department_exists: "<fixture>",
):
    dept = ensure_department_exists(name="Dept DT Unauth")
    dept_id = dept["id"]
    payload = {"name": "Should Fail Unauth", "cost_center": "CC-003"}

    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/api/departments/{dept_id}",
        json=payload,
        # No Authorization header supplied
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_update_department_returns_403
preconditions: A user exists with an employee role (non-manager) and valid credentials
A department with known id exists
actions: Obtain an auth token for a non-manager (employee-role) user via <get_employee_token>
Create or retrieve a target department via <ensure_department_exists> using an admin token
PATCH /api/departments/{id} using the employee token with a valid payload
Verify the department record is unchanged via GET /api/departments/{id} using an admin token
Verify no audit record was written for this attempted update
expected: No audit record is written for the forbidden attempt
trace: R-WEB-006: precondition role:manager must be satisfied
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_forbidden_role_update_department_returns_403(
    employee_token: str,
    admin_token: str,
    api_client: "<fixture>",
    ensure_department_exists: "<fixture>",
    get_latest_audit_record: "<helper_fn>",
):
    dept = ensure_department_exists(name="Dept EP Forbidden Role")
    dept_id = dept["id"]
    original_name = dept["name"]
    payload = {"name": "Should Be Forbidden", "cost_center": "CC-004"}

    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/api/departments/{dept_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_token}"},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"

    # Confirm the department was not modified
    get_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/api/departments/{dept_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"},
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json().get("name") == original_name, "Department should not have been modified"

    # Confirm no audit record was written for this forbidden attempt
    audit = get_latest_audit_record(entity="department", entity_id=dept_id)
    assert audit is None or audit.get("action") != "update" or audit.get("actor_role") != "employee", \
        "No audit record should exist for a forbidden update attempt"
